summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Earthquakes & Seismic Waves
summary_sections.content_html:
An earthquake is a sudden release of energy in Earth's lithosphere caused by movement along faults.

  • Focus (hypocenter): the point underground where the earthquake originates
  • Epicenter: the point on the surface directly above the focus
  • Seismic waves:
    • P-waves (primary): fastest, compressional, travel through solids and liquids
    • S-waves (secondary): slower, shear motion, travel through solids only
    • Surface waves: slowest but most destructive, travel along Earth's surface
  • Richter scale (magnitude): measures energy released; each whole number = 10× amplitude, ~32× energy
  • Most earthquakes occur along plate boundaries, especially the Ring of Fire around the Pacific.
